subject: Statistics
title: Conjugate Priors
description: Learn Conjugate Priors in one minute.

hook.text: What if choosing the right prior made the math effortless? Conjugate priors do exactly that.

buildup.text: A conjugate prior produces a posterior in the same mathematical family. Beta in, beta out. Normal in, normal out.

discovery.text: This means you can update beliefs with just arithmetic — no complex computation needed.

twist.text: Conjugate priors are mathematically convenient, not always realistic. You sacrifice accuracy for simplicity.

climax.text: Before computers, conjugate priors were the only practical option. Now MCMC handles any prior shape.

punchline.text: The prior that makes the math easy. Elegant but limiting.

quiz.question: What is a conjugate prior?
options: A prior that produces a posterior in the same mathematical family, making updates simple
A prior that equals the posterior exactly
